evals.prompt: I want to build a chess app where players can create rooms and games are saved in Cyoda Cloud
evals.expected_output: Gives brief Cyoda orientation, identifies Room/Game/Move as entities, asks about states and transitions one question at a time, presents structured design summary without forcing compute nodes

assertions.text: Gives brief EDBMS/entity/workflow orientation for newcomer
Asks only one question at a time
Identifies Room, Game, Move as likely entities
Asks about states for each entity
Presents compute nodes as optional, not required
Produces a structured design summary
Does NOT generate workflow JSON before design is confirmed

evals.prompt: I need to model a risk assessment entity with states for pending, under_review, and approved. It needs a scheduled timeout.
evals.expected_output: Skips orientation (user knows Cyoda), asks clarifying questions about transitions, recognises scheduled timeout pattern from patterns.md, asks about compute node needs

assertions.text: Does NOT give Cyoda orientation to an experienced user
Asks clarifying questions about transitions and triggers
Recognises the scheduled timeout pattern and references it
Asks about compute node needs

evals.prompt: I want a simple document approval workflow — nothing fancy
evals.expected_output: Asks about domain objects and states, presents compute nodes as optional, accepts 'no compute nodes needed' gracefully, produces design summary without compute nodes. When asking Q5 about schema mode, explains discover vs lock with the ability to switch later.

assertions.text: Asks about domain objects and their states
Presents compute nodes as optional when asking about them
Accepts 'no compute nodes needed' gracefully without pushing back
Does NOT include compute nodes in design summary when not needed
Q5 explains discover mode: schema evolves automatically, no upfront definitions
Q5 explains lock mode: schema fixed, mismatches rejected
Q5 mentions you can start with discover and switch to lock later

evals.prompt: I want to model an Order entity with these states: draft, submitted, processing, calling_payment_api, retry, approved, rejected
evals.expected_output: Removes technical states (processing, calling_payment_api, retry) during Phase 3 design review, explains each removal inline, produces corrected design summary with business states only (draft, submitted, approved, rejected), references design-guidelines.md for async processor pattern

assertions.text: Removes processing, calling_payment_api, and retry as technical states
Briefly explains each removal inline in plain language
Produces design summary with business states only: draft, submitted, approved, rejected
References design-guidelines.md for the async processor pattern
Does NOT silently drop states without explanation
